title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They are going to renovate the old library next year.
questions.secondSentence: The city council plans (1) _________ next year.
solutions: to have the old library renovated

questions.firstSentence: ‘I'll help you with the project,’ said David.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs followed by an infinitive.
solutions: offered to give me a hand

questions.firstSentence: I didn't listen to her, so I made a huge mistake.
questions.explanation: Tests third conditional for past hypothetical situations.
questions.secondSentence: I wouldn't have made a huge mistake (1) _________ to her.

questions.firstSentence: I'm certain they were very disappointed by the results.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for certain deduction in the past.

questions.firstSentence: You should never reveal your password to anyone.
questions.explanation: Tests inversion with negative prepositional phrases (Under no circumstances).
questions.secondSentence: Under (1) _________ reveal your password to anyone.

questions.firstSentence: It's too cold to go swimming today.
questions.explanation: Tests 'not + adjective + enough' as an alternative to 'too + adjective'.
questions.secondSentence: It is (1) _________ to go swimming today.
